title: Conchidium summerhayesianum
taxonomy: Conchidium summerhayesianum is classified as a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family is commonly known as the filmy fern family due to the delicate, filmy texture of their fronds. The Hymenophyllaceae family is one of the seven families in the order Hymenophytaceae. Within the Hymenophyllaceae family, Conchidium summerhayesianum is one of the many species that are endemic to Southeast Asia. The species is also sometimes classified under the genus Pseudodrynaria.

intro: Conchidium summerhayesianum, also known as Summerhayes' Conchidium, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Conchidium summerhayesianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
